qas.q: Liquid surge is caused by:
qas.a: movement of the liquid product in the cargo tank

qas.q: Knowing the outage needed for the liquids is important because:
qas.a: some liquids expand more than others when they get warm

qas.q: What will liquid surge do to the handling of a tank vehicle?
qas.a: it can move the vehicle in the same direction the liquid moves

qas.q: When your cargo tank has bulkheads, what difference in handling can you expect?
qas.a: there will be less front to back surge than for a smooth bore

qas.q: Knowing your center of gravity is important when hauling liquids. It is best to keep the center of gravity:
qas.a: low

qas.q: Surge is generally:
qas.a: front to back and side to side

qas.q: How would you expect a truck with a baffled cargo tank to handle on the road?
qas.a: there will be less front-to-back surge than there is in tankers without baffles

qas.q: When loading a cargo tank with liquid, you must consider which of the following?
qas.a: the legal weight limits in the states in which you will be driving, the amount the liquid will expand during transit, the weight of the liquid

qas.q: You are driving a truck and the front wheels begin to skid. Which of these is most likely to occur?
qas.a: you will continue in a straight line and keep moving forward no matter how much you steer.

qas.q: A liquid tank with baffles can still have what kind of surge?
qas.a: side to side

qas.q: You are driving a loaded tank vehicle and are exiting a highway using an off-ramp that curves downhill. You should:
qas.a: slow down to a safe speed before the turn

qas.q: A smooth bore tank which is loaded to 50% capacity will:
qas.a: have more surge

qas.q: A smooth bore tank will:
qas.a: have more surge than a baffled tank

qas.q: You need to be extremely cautious when driving smooth bore tankers. This is especially true when you are:
qas.a: starting or stopping

qas.q: Which of the following is important to remember in an emergency?
qas.a: heavy vehicles can almost always turn more quickly than they can stop

qas.q: When you unload the smaller tanks of a tank with bulkheads, be careful to check your:
qas.a: weight distribution

qas.q: Which fires can you use water to put out?
qas.a: Tire fires

qas.q: Which of these statements about emergency steering and tankers is true?
qas.a: when making a quick steering movement, do not apply the brakes

qas.q: Which of these is a good rule to follow when using a fire extinguisher?
qas.a: stay upwind of the fire, and aim at the base of the fire

qas.q: Most tankers have a high center of gravity. Which of the following is important to remember when pulling a liquid or dry bulk tanker?
qas.a: tankers can roll over at the speed limits posted for curves, most tankers have a high center of gravity, curves, exit and entrance ramps have speed limits which are safe for cars, but may be to fast for tankers

qas.q: Which of these can cause a vehicle to skid?
qas.a: oversteering, overbraking, overacceleration

qas.q: You are driving a tank vehicle and you must stop quickly to avoid a crash. You should:
qas.a: use control or stab braking

qas.q: You should know the outage needed for the liquids you carry because:
qas.a: some liquids expand more than others when they get warm

qas.q: Side-to-side surge can cause:
qas.a: rollover

qas.q: When should tanker drivers who have lost their brakes use truck escape ramps?
qas.a: always

qas.q: There are two lists that drivers, shippers, and carriers use to find out if a material is a regulated product. Which of the following is one of them?
qas.a: List of Hazardous Substances and Reportable Quantities

qas.q: A vehicle placarded for hazardous materials is required to have placards on how many sides of the vehicle?
A hazard class name or ID number must not be used to describe a:
qas.a: non-hazardous material

qas.q: The intention of the Hazardous Materials regulations are to ensure safe drivers and equipment, to communicate the risk, and?
qas.a: to contain the material

qas.q: If an X or an RQ is written or typed into the HM column of a shipping paper entry, the:
qas.a: Shipment is regulated by hazardous material regulations

qas.q: Who do the regulations say is responsible for packaging, labeling, and preparing the hazardous material shipping papers for a common carrier?
qas.a: Shipper

qas.q: A power unit of a placarded vehicle is required to have a fire extinguisher with a UL rating of _____ B:C or more.
You may not park a vehicle carrying hazardous materials within how many feet of an open fire?
Which emergency equipment may be used to warn of a stopped vehicle which contains explosives?
qas.a: reflective triangles

qas.q: The description of a hazardous product must include the hazard class, the identification number, and its proper shipping name. Which is required to be listed first on the shipping papers?
qas.a: the proper shipping name

qas.q: When shippers package hazardous materials, they must certify that this was done according to the regulations. The one exception to the rule is when:
qas.a: the shipper is a private carrier carrying their own product

qas.q: When you are transporting chlorine in cargo tanks, you must have:
qas.a: An approved gas mask, an emergency kit for controlling leaks in fittings on the dome cover plate

qas.q: Carriers are required to give each driver who transports Class A or B explosives:
qas.a: a copy of FMCSR part 397

qas.q: When you are handling packages of explosives, you must:
qas.a: never use hooks or other metal tools

qas.q: If the word "Forbidden" appears in the hazard class column of a Hazardous Materials table entry:
qas.a: you must never transport the product

qas.q: What is the largest allowable total transport index of all packages in a single vehicle allowed to be
You are hauling 2000 pounds of a corrosive. You may park within ______ feet of the road if your work requires it and it is for a very short period of time.
If you are hauling hazardous materials and you discover that one of your tires is leaking. You must:
qas.a: stop at the nearest safe place and fix it

qas.q: You should stop before crossing a railroad grade if your vehicle is carrying how many gallons of chlorine?
qas.a: any amount

qas.q: Animal and human foodstuffs must not be loaded in the same vehicle with
qas.a: poisons

qas.q: When fueling a placarded vehicle, someone must always be:
qas.a: at the nozzle, controlling the fuel flow

qas.q: Who do the regulations say is responsible for checking that the shipper correctly named, labeled, and marked a hazardous material shipment?
qas.a: Carrier

qas.q: While transporting hazardous material you stop to eat lunch at a truck stop. While you are in the truck stop, the shipping papers must be placed on the drivers seat or:
qas.a: in the driver's door pouch

qas.q: During an enroute inspection, you discover an overheated tire. If you are hauling hazardous materials, you must
qas.a: remove the tire and place it a safe distance from the vehicle

qas.q: When hauling hazardous materials, you must stop your vehicle and check any dual tires at least once every ___. Whichever is less.
qas.a: 2 hours or 100 miles

qas.q: During a hazardous materials emergency, you must use every available means to
qas.a: keep people away, prevent smoking and keep open flame away, warn others of the danger

qas.q: If you are transporting Explosives A you must not
qas.a: transport them in vehicle combinations if there is a placarded cargo tank in the combination

qas.q: If hazardous material is leaking from your vehicle, you must not move your vehicle:
qas.a: any more than safety requires

qas.q: The transport index of a radioactive material, tells you:
qas.a: tells the degree of control needed during transportation

qas.q: Only one of these shipping paper descriptions for a hazardous material is in the correct order. Select the correct one.
qas.a: Hydrogen Bromide, non-flammable gas, UN 1048

qas.q: A "W" in the first column of the Hazardous Materials Table indicates:
qas.a: the entry applies to water shipments that are not a RQ

qas.q: You may not smoke around any vehicle being loaded or unloaded with:
qas.a: flammables, oxidizers, explosives

qas.q: If a vehicle carrying explosives crashes with another object, you must not separate the vehicle from the other object until:
qas.a: someone has removed the explosives and placed them at least 200 feet away

qas.q: Before loading or unloading any explosive, you must check the cargo space for:
qas.a: a cargo heater that could start, sharp points that might damage the cargo, loose floor boards or plates

qas.q: A liquid poison is spilling from your vehicle. If it can be done, safely, how should you channel the liquid?
qas.a: away from streams or sewers
